creator: olgeni@FreeBSD.org
text:
8.1-RELEASE i386 on a PICMG motherboard produces a "driver bug:
Unable to set devclass (devname: (null))" each time the ISA bus is
probed (kldload of a custom device driver).

After poking in the custom driver it appears that the message is
actually related to the fdc driver.

Setting bootverbose gives the following dmesg snippet:

===
fdc0 failed to probe at port 0x3f0-0x3f5,0x3f7 irq 6 drq 2 on isa0
fdc: fdc-1 already exists; skipping it
driver bug: Unable to set devclass (name: fdc, devname: (null))
===

After booting, the floppy device does not work (no /dev/fd0).

The "driver bug" issue is still present when using an unpatched
GENERIC kernel without anything custom loaded, and seems related
to the probe failure.

Full log follows:

How-To-Repeat: The following patch to sys/kern/subr_bus.c allows at least to point
out the driver responsible for the null devname:

--- subr_bus.c.orig	2010-08-18 21:21:17.000000000 +0200
+++ subr_bus.c	2010-08-18 21:21:56.000000000 +0200
@@ -1951,7 +1951,8 @@
 			device_set_driver(child, dl->driver);
 			if (!hasclass) {
 				if (device_set_devclass(child, dl->driver->name)) {
-					printf("driver bug: Unable to set devclass (devname: %s)\n",
+					printf("driver bug: Unable to set devclass (name: %s, devname: %s)\n",
+					    dl->driver->name,
 					    (child ? device_get_name(child) :
 						"no device"));
 					device_set_driver(child, NULL);
